The '''69th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[BAFTAs]], were held on 14 February 2016 at the [[Royal Opera House]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2015 in film|2015]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bafta.org/press/press-releases/film-awards-key-dates-and-rules |title=EE British Academy Film Awards: Key Dates and Rules Published |date=5 August 2015 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |access-date=12 October 2015}}</ref> Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bafta.org/film/awards/film-awards-information |title=EE British Academy Film Awards information |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |access-date=12 October 2015}}</ref>

The nominations were announced on 8 January 2016 by [[Stephen Fry]] and actress [[Gugu Mbatha-Raw]]. ''[[Bridge of Spies (film)|Bridge of Spies]]'' and ''[[Carol (film)|Carol]]'' received the most nominations at nine each.<ref name="Barraclough">{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2015/film/awards/bafta-reveals-key-dates-for-film-awards-to-be-held-in-2016-1201556893/ |title=BAFTA Reveals Key Dates for Film Awards to Be Held in 2016 |last=Barraclough |first=Leo |date=5 August 2015 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |access-date=12 October 2015}}</ref><ref name="BBC News">{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-35260399 |title=Bafta Film Awards 2016: Winners |date=14 February 2016 |work=[[BBC News]] |access-date=15 February 2016}}</ref> Despite leading the field in nominations, ''Carol'' failed to win any awards and ''Bridge of Spies'' won just one; [[Mark Rylance]] for [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Supporting Role|Best Supporting Actor]].

''[[The Revenant (2015 film)|The Revenant]]'' won the most awards at the ceremony with five, including [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Direction|Best Director]] for [[Alejandro González Iñárritu|Alejandro G. Iñárritu]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role|Best Actor]] for [[Leonardo DiCaprio]], and [[BAFTA Award for Best Cinematography|Best Cinematography]] for [[Emmanuel Lubezki]]. [[Brie Larson]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role|Best Actress]] for ''[[Room (2015 film)|Room]]'' and [[Kate Winslet]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Supporting Role|Best Supporting Actress]] for ''[[Steve Jobs (film)|Steve Jobs]]''. ''[[Mad Max: Fury Road]]'' won four awards; [[BAFTA Award for Best Costume Design|Best Costume Design]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Editing|Best Editing]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Makeup and Hair|Best Makeup and Hair]], and [[BAFTA Award for Best Production Design|Best Production Design]]. ''[[Brooklyn (film)|Brooklyn]]'', directed by [[John Crowley (director)|John Crowley]], was voted [[BAFTA Award for Outstanding British Film|Outstanding British Film]] of 2015. [[Sidney Poitier]] was awarded the [[BAFTA Fellowship]] for his contribution to cinema.<ref name="Lodderhose">{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2016/film/news/bafta-awards-winners-2016-complete-list-british-academy-film-television-1201706004/ |title='The Revenant,' Leonardo DiCaprio Dominate BAFTA Awards |last=Lodderhose |first=Diana |date=14 February 2016 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |access-date=15 February 2016}}</ref>

The ceremony was broadcast on [[BBC One]] and was watched by 4.5 million viewers, down from 4.9 million in 2015 and the lowest television audience since 2010.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/media/2016/feb/15/bafta-ceremony-pulls-in-lowest-tv-audience-in-six-years |title=Bafta ceremony pulls in lowest TV audience in six years |last=Revoir |first=Paul |date=15 February 2016 |work=[[The Guardian]] |access-date=15 February 2016}}</ref>

==Ceremony information==
The ceremony was broadcast on [[BBC One]] at 9 p.m. [[Greenwich Mean Time|GMT]], around two hours later than the actual ceremony. For the eleventh time, [[Stephen Fry]] acted as the host. The ceremony commenced with a segment commemorating the year in film, which was accompanied by the song [["Heroes" (David Bowie song)|"Heroes"]] by [[David Bowie]], who had died the previous month. Highlighting that the ceremony was being held on [[Valentine's Day]], the ceremony showed a [[kiss cam]] where random celebrities such as [[Maggie Smith|Dame Maggie Smith]] and [[Leonardo DiCaprio]] were persuaded by Fry to kiss each other.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/filmblog/2016/feb/15/baftas-2016-10-things-we-learned |title=Baftas 2016: 10 things we learned |last1=Lee |first1=Benjamin |last2=Bradshaw |first2=Peter |last3=Beech |first3=Peter |last4=Shoard |first4=Catherine |date=15 February 2016 |work=[[The Guardian]] |access-date=15 February 2016}}</ref> Fry also received a number of negative comments after he said of [[BAFTA Award for Best Costume Design|Best Costume Design]] winner [[Jenny Beavan]] (''[[Mad Max: Fury Road]]'') as "Only one of the great cinematic costume designers would come to an awards ceremony dressed as a [[wikt:bag lady|bag lady]]". He subsequently deleted his [[Twitter]] account following criticism of his joke.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2016/feb/14/stephen-fry-hits-back-at-criticism-of-baftas-bag-lady-joke-she-got-it-derrr |title=Stephen Fry hits back at criticism of Baftas 'bag lady' joke: 'She got it. Derrr' |last=Lee |first=Benjamin |date=14 February 2016 |work=[[The Guardian]] |access-date=15 February 2016}}</ref>

A number of presenters referred to the [[88th Academy Awards#Criticism regarding lack of diversity|lack of diversity at the Academy Awards]]. [[Rebel Wilson]] and [[Sacha Baron Cohen]] both made jokes referring to the controversy.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/films/news/baftas-2016-rebel-wilson-and-sacha-baron-cohens-presenter-speeches-take-on-diversity-debate-a6874191.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160216001614/http://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/films/news/baftas-2016-rebel-wilson-and-sacha-baron-cohens-presenter-speeches-take-on-diversity-debate-a6874191.html |archive-date=2016-02-16 |url-access=limited |url-status=live |title=BAFTAs 2016: Rebel Wilson and Sacha Baron Cohen's speeches tackle diversity debate |last=Loughrey |first=Clarisse |date=15 February 2016 |work=[[The Independent]] |access-date=15 February 2016}}</ref> [[Sidney Poitier]] was not present to collect his fellowship due to ill health.<ref name="Lodderhose" /> [[Jamie Foxx]] and Poitier's daughter, [[Sydney Tamiia Poitier]], presented the award to him in person at his home in [[Los Angeles]]. [[Noel Clarke]], [[Lulu (singer)|Lulu]], and [[Oprah Winfrey]] paid tribute to him in a filmed segment.

The ''In Memoriam'' section featured [[Alan Rickman]], [[Melissa Mathison]], [[Andrew Lesnie]], [[Maureen O'Hara]], Gayle Griffiths, [[Haskell Wexler]], [[Colin Welland]], [[James Horner]], [[David Bowie]], [[Ron Moody]], [[June Randall]], [[Julie Harris (costume designer)|Julie Harris]], [[Frank Finlay]], [[Philip French]], [[Vilmos Zsigmond]], [[Albert and David Maysles|Albert Maysles]], [[Richard Johnson (actor)|Richard Johnson]], [[Wes Craven]], [[Jacques Rivette]], [[Penelope Houston]], [[Tommie Manderson]], [[Christopher Wood (writer)|Christopher Wood]], [[Saeed Jaffrey]], [[Sheila Sim]] and [[Christopher Lee|Sir Christopher Lee]].
